dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic was the only tracked provider with meaningful front-page discussion, but that attention came from a widely upvoted Claude security story focused on prompt-injection-driven file exfiltration, making it the day's winner by default despite a clearly negative tone [E1].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic led only because no other tracked provider had meaningful direct story-and-comment signal today. The dominant HN conversation was about Claude Cowork exfiltrating files, with commenters describing the issue as a basic prompt-injection problem and criticizing Claude's implicit skill model and trust boundaries [E1]. That gave Anthropic the most coverage, but not a good day.

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude drew intense criticism over a front-page security story showing file exfiltration via prompt injection, with comments arguing the attack was basic and that Claude's implicit skill model made abuse easier [E1].

evidence.summary: Front-page story 'Claude Cowork exfiltrates files' generated heavy discussion centered on Claude security weaknesses; top comments called it a basic prompt-injection attack, warned Claude's implicit 'skills' model makes abuse easier, and argued these systems need much clearer trust boundaries.
